steps.title: Solve the constants directly
steps.content: Because the equation has infinitely many solutions, both sides must simplify to the same linear expression. Match the $x$-coefficient first, then match the constant term.

steps.title: Check the coefficient condition
steps.content: Simplify the left side: $$\frac{12x+28}{4}-\frac{s}{13}=3x+7-\frac{s}{13}.$$ The right side is $rx-8r$. For the two sides to be identical, the $x$-coefficients must match, so $r=3$.

steps.title: Use the constant condition
steps.content: Now match constants using $r=3$: $$7-\frac{s}{13}=-8(3)=-24$$ $$-\frac{s}{13}=-31$$ $$s=403$$ With $s=403$, the left side becomes $3x+7-31=3x-24$, and the right side is $3(x-8)=3x-24$. The answer is 403.
